facts.value: Trained in the practice of believing nothing he has not derived from first principles

value.claim: The reasoning 'I behaved as if I saw her, therefore I saw her' is a valid derivation
value.correct_statement: It is a tautology, not a derivation; a first-year student would catch it

value.claim: He catches the tautology in his own self-assessment of attentiveness
value.correct_statement: He does not catch this one because his rigor stays inside the discipline

facts.value: Mathematician (has spent a career catching tautologies in other domains)
Applies rigor only inside mathematics; outside the discipline he uses a different, sloppier procedure and does not notice the discrepancy
Accepts the tautology 'I behaved as if I saw her, therefore I saw her' as proof of his own attentiveness

behavioral_states.response: Applies strict first-principles rigor; catches tautologies

behavioral_states.trigger: Reasoning outside mathematics (esp. about his own attentiveness / her)
behavioral_states.response: Applies a different, sloppy procedure; accepts tautologies he would catch elsewhere; does not notice the discrepancy
behavioral_states.observable: Self-justifying tautology: 'I behaved as if I saw her, therefore I saw her'

properties.note: Mistakes behaving as if he saw her for actually seeing her; identity of 'her' unspecified in this excerpt

negations.claim: The reasoning 'I behaved as if I saw her, therefore I saw her' is a valid derivation
negations.correct_statement: It is a tautology, not a derivation; a first-year student would catch it

negations.claim: He catches the tautology in his own self-assessment of attentiveness
negations.correct_statement: He does not catch this one because his rigor stays inside the discipline
